summary: Extend mock classes in selection.test.ts: add `sub(v)`, `multiplyScalar(s)` to MockVector3; add `getWorldDirection(target)` to MockPerspectiveCamera. These are needed for V-02/V-03 tests but should be added upfront to avoid mixing mock infrastructure changes with test logic.; V-10: Write failing tests for click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.test.ts. New describe block 'click-vs-drag discrimination' with tests: (1) pointerdown alone does NOT fire onSelect, (2) pointerdown+pointerup at same position fires onSelect with raycasted entity, (3) pointerdown+pointerup with >5px movement does NOT fire onSelect, (4) pointerdown+pointerup with <=5px movement fires onSelect, (5) onSelect receives null when pointerup raycast misses, (6) after dispose, pointerup does not fire onSelect.; V-10: Implement click-vs-drag discrimination in selection.ts. Replace `handlePointerDown` (which fires onSelect immediately) with: (a) `handlePointerDown` that records {clientX, clientY} and adds a one-shot pointerup listener, (b) `handlePointerUp` that computes Euclidean distance from pointerdown position and only calls raycast()+onSelect() if distance < 5px. Update dispose() to also remove pointerup listener. Update existing 'click-based selection raycasting' tests and 'rAF-throttled pointermove / pointerdown still raycasts synchronously' test to dispatch both pointerdown+pointerup events instead of just pointerdown.; V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for fitToView with view-direction offset and controls.target update. In selection.test.ts, add tests: (1) fitToView offsets camera along current view direction (not just Z) — set camera at an angle, verify position is center - viewDir*distance, (2) fitToView updates controls.target to bounding box center when controls provided via SelectionOptions, (3) fitToView still works without controls (backward compat). Update setup() to optionally accept a controls mock with a target Vector3.; V-02/V-03: Fix fitToView in selection.ts. Add optional `controls?: { target: { copy: (v: any) => void } }` field to SelectionOptions interface. In fitToView(): (a) get view direction via `camera.getWorldDirection(dir)`, (b) position camera at `center - viewDir * distance` instead of just Z offset, (c) if controls provided, set `controls.target.copy(center)`. Update existing fitToView tests that asserted Z-only offset to match new view-direction behavior.; V-02/V-03: Write failing tests for flyToEntity with view-direction offset and controls.target update. Tests: (1) flyToEntity offsets camera along current view direction, (2) flyToEntity updates controls.target to entity bounding box center, (3) flyToEntity with unknown entity does not crash or update controls.target.; V-02/V-03: Fix flyToEntity in selection.ts. Apply same view-direction offset and controls.target update pattern as fitToView. Update existing flyToEntity tests that asserted Z-only offset.

Completed final two steps: step-7 test was already written by prior iteration but uncommitted (confirmed it fails correctly), committed it. step-8 added `controls: controls.controls` to createSelection call in Viewport.tsx to thread OrbitControls through for orbit target updates. All 70 tests pass (56 selection + 14 Viewport).

Write failing test in meshManager.test.ts: when data.normals is null, createMeshFromData calls geometry.computeVertexNormals() (V-04). Add computeVertexNormals mock to MockBufferGeometry. Test: sync with null normals, assert computeVertexNormals was called. Also test: sync with provided normals, assert computeVertexNormals was NOT called.; Implement V-04 in meshManager.ts: In createMeshFromData, add else branch after the normals check — when data.normals is null, call geometry.computeVertexNormals(). In updateMeshGeometry, in the else-if branch where normals are removed (line 97-98), call geometry.computeVertexNormals() after deleteAttribute.; Write failing test in meshManager.test.ts: material is created with side: THREE.DoubleSide (V-05). Add DoubleSide constant to mock. Assert that the material's side property equals DoubleSide (2).; Implement V-05 in meshManager.ts: Import DoubleSide from 'three'. Add side: DoubleSide to the MeshStandardMaterial constructor options.; Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts for mesh data validation (V-06). Tests: (1) sync with vertices.length not divisible by 3 does not add mesh to scene, (2) sync with an index >= vertex count does not add mesh, (3) valid data still works normally. Also test update path: update with invalid data skips the update.; Implement V-06 in meshManager.ts: Add validateMeshData(data: MeshData): boolean that checks vertices.length % 3 === 0 and max(indices) < vertices.length / 3. Guard createMeshFromData and updateMeshGeometry calls in sync() with this validation, logging a console.warn for invalid data.; Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts for safe buffer updates when array length changes (V-07). Tests: (1) update with same-length arrays reuses existing BufferAttribute (identity check — existing test), (2) update with different-length vertex array creates a new BufferAttribute (not same reference), (3) update with different-length index array creates new index BufferAttribute, (4) new BufferAttribute has correct array and count.; Implement V-07 in meshManager.ts: In updateMeshGeometry, before reusing posAttr, check if posAttr.array.length !== data.vertices.length — if so, create new BufferAttribute via setAttribute instead of mutating. Same logic for index and normals.

Steps 9-15 were already implemented by prior iteration but plan.json was not updated. Verified all tests pass. Step 16: added refreshSelected to createSelection return object, added selection.refreshSelected() call after meshManager.sync() in Viewport.tsx, updated Viewport test mock. All 119 viewport tests pass.

Verify vitest and @solidjs/testing-library work in the worktree by running existing Toast and ReloadPrompt tests; Write failing test for TO-4: Toast close button should have aria-label='Close'. Assert that the button inside the toast has the correct aria-label attribute.; Add aria-label='Close' to the x button in Toast.tsx; Write failing test for TO-3: Toast root div should have role='alert' and aria-live='assertive'. Assert both attributes exist on the toast element.; Add role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' to the toast root div in Toast.tsx; Write failing tests for TO-1: Toast auto-dismiss. Use vi.useFakeTimers. Tests: (1) success toast calls onDismiss after 3000ms, (2) error toast calls onDismiss after 5000ms, (3) info toast calls onDismiss after 3000ms, (4) manual dismiss before timeout does not double-call onDismiss (clearTimeout on cleanup).; Add auto-dismiss setTimeout in Toast.tsx using onMount/onCleanup. Compute timeout from props.type (3000ms for success/info, 5000ms for error). Call props.onDismiss() on timeout. Clear timeout in onCleanup.; Write failing test for TO-5: Toast should have a CSS animation applied. Verify the toast element's computed/applied class includes the animation styling (check for animation-name or that the CSS module class is applied correctly).; Add @keyframes slideIn animation to Toast.module.css (translateX(100%) + opacity 0 -> translateX(0) + opacity 1 over 200ms ease-out). Apply animation property to .toast class.; Write failing tests for TO-2: Toast queue support in App. Tests: (1) showToast adds a ToastMessage to the toasts array, (2) two sequential showToast calls result in two toasts rendered, (3) dismissing one toast by id removes only that toast, (4) handleDoExport success creates a toast in the queue. This requires refactoring App.tsx toast state from single signal to array.; In App.tsx: Replace toastMessage/toastType signals with a toasts signal of ToastMessage[]. Add showToast(message, type) helper that generates unique id (counter-based) and appends. Update handleDismissToast to accept id and filter. Update handleDoExport to use showToast(). Render with <For> over toasts array, each rendering a <Toast>. Update Toast props to accept ToastMessage (id + message + type). Update App.module.css toastContainer to stack with flex-direction: column-reverse and gap: 8px.

Completed all remaining steps. Steps 11-12 (R-1 ReloadPrompt multi-file) were committed by prior iteration. Step 13 (R-1 App integration tests) was committed prior. Step 14: fixed App.tsx handleReload/handleDismissReload/JSX to use changedFiles Set properly. Steps 15-16: added dirty-file check before reload with confirmation flow — captured editorStore from Editor mock, handleReload checks intersection with dirtyFiles, sets confirmReload signal, ReloadPrompt shows warning + Reload Anyway. Steps 17-18: added Ctrl+Shift+R and Escape keyboard shortcuts to useKeyboardShortcuts hook, wired in App.tsx. All 71 tests pass across 4 test files.

Write failing tests for R-1: ReloadPrompt multi-file tracking. Tests: (1) renders nothing when filePaths is empty array, (2) renders single file basename when filePaths has one entry, (3) renders 'N files changed on disk' when filePaths has 2+ entries, (4) Reload and Dismiss buttons still work.; Update ReloadPrompt props from filePath: string|null to filePaths: string[]. Show when array is non-empty. Display basename for single file, 'N files changed on disk. Reload?' for multiple. Update existing tests to use new prop name.; Write failing tests for R-1 App integration: In App.tsx, changedFiles should be a Set<string> signal. Tests: (1) onFileChanged with two different files adds both to the set, (2) onFileChanged with same file twice only has one entry, (3) handleReload reloads all files in set, (4) handleDismissReload clears the set. Update existing App.test.tsx tests that reference changedFile/filePath.; In App.tsx: Convert changedFile signal to changedFiles as createSignal<Set<string>>(new Set()). Update onFileChanged handler to add to set via new Set([...prev, path]). Pass Array.from(changedFiles()) as filePaths to ReloadPrompt. Update handleReload to iterate all files in set, calling bridgeOpenFile for each. Update handleDismissReload to reset to empty set. Fix any broken existing App tests.; Write failing tests for R-4: dirty-file check before reload. Tests: (1) when no dirty files overlap, handleReload proceeds immediately (bridgeOpenFile called), (2) when dirty files overlap with changed files, handleReload shows a confirmation warning (does NOT call bridgeOpenFile immediately), (3) confirming 'Reload Anyway' triggers bridgeOpenFile for all changed files, (4) dismissing confirmation does not reload.; In App.tsx handleReload: Check intersection of changedFiles with editorStore.state.dirtyFiles. If overlap exists, set a confirmReload signal to true. In ReloadPrompt: accept optional hasDirtyFiles prop. When true, show warning text 'Unsaved changes will be lost' and change Reload button text to 'Reload Anyway'. When false or absent, show normal UI. In App.tsx, pass hasDirtyFiles computed from intersection. handleReload only proceeds to bridgeOpenFile when confirmReload is set or no dirty overlap. Update ReloadPrompt.module.css with warning styling.; Write failing tests for R-2: keyboard shortcuts. Tests: (1) Ctrl+Shift+R dispatched on document calls onReloadShortcut callback, (2) Escape dispatched on document calls onDismissReload callback, (3) shortcuts are skipped when target is an input element.; Extend KeyboardShortcutCallbacks in useKeyboardShortcuts.ts with onReloadShortcut and onDismissReload. Add handlers: Ctrl+Shift+R -> onReloadShortcut, Escape -> onDismissReload. In App.tsx, wire onReloadShortcut to handleReload (only when changedFiles non-empty) and onDismissReload to handleDismissReload (only when reload prompt visible).

Step 19: Added 5 failing tests for partial_failure_state_corruption in handleReload — tests verify that Promise.allSettled correctly partitions succeeded/failed files, resets confirmReload, shows error toast on failure, and preserves existing all-succeed behavior. Step 20: Replaced Promise.all with Promise.allSettled in handleReload. Failed files remain in changedFiles, succeeded files are removed, error toast shown for failures, confirmReload always reset. All 45 App tests pass. 1 pre-existing failure in panels-smoke.test.tsx (unrelated).
